title: daucus anthriscus
intro: Daucus anthriscus, also known as Queen Anne's Lace, is a biennial flowering plant in the carrot family that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Daucus anthriscus include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of Queen Anne's Lace can be found in teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
